perturbation.original_text: As used herein, the following words have the indicated meanings: (i) "Disclose" means to reveal, make known, make available, furnish, or permit access to, whether or not intentionally.
perturbation.changed_text: As used herein, the following words have the indicated meanings: (i) "Disseminate" means to reveal, make known, make available, furnish, or permit access to, whether or not intentionally.
perturbation.explanation: Changing the defined term from "Disclose" to "Disseminate" without updating the rest of the document, where the term "Disclose" is used repeatedly, causes a contradiction. The contract then uses "Disclose" without its defined meaning, which can lead to disputes over the intended scope of the non-disclosure obligations. This error is further compounded when the rest of the document continues to use the term `Disclose`. The definition of disseminate is not being used because the contract makes no further mentions of this term.
perturbation.contradicted_text: As used herein, the following words have the indicated meanings: (i) "Disseminate" means to reveal, make known, make available, furnish, or permit access to, whether or not intentionally. (ii) "Information" means all oral, written, or other information whatsoever, including information in documents and other recording media and information embodied in any item, which in connection with the Matter, is (a) obtained by Interested Party from or through Disclosing Party, (b) obtained by or through Interested Party by an examination of any Item, or (c) created by or through Interested Party with the use of information in (a) or (b). It includes but is not limited to ideas, inventions, discoveries, formulas, methods, designs, drawings, specifications, engineering and manufacturing data. This information is limited to trade secrets and other proprietary or private information of Disclosing Party or of any third party if disclosed by or through Disclosing Party. (iii) "Item" means any system, subsystem, assembly, subassembly, device, components, product, or machine, work of authorship, or part thereof, or substance which is disclosed by or through Disclosing Party hereunder, which embodies trade secret or other proprietary or private information of Disclosing Party or of any third party if disclosed by or through Disclosing Party. (iv) "Matter" means the project or other matter in connection with which this Agreement is executed. This matter is or relates to the potential acquisition by the Interested Party of a controlling ownership interest in the share capital or business of the Disclosing Party. The parties to this Agreement have agreed as follows: 1. Non-Disclosure. Interested Party agrees as follows: 1.1. For a period of three (3) years from the date of entering in to this Agreement, not to disclosure any Information or Item, in any manner whatsoever, directly or indirectly, whether in whole or in part, without the prior written consent of Disclosing Party, to any individual, firm or entity, excepting solely an individual <PAGE> 2 who (a) is an officer, director or employee of Disclosing Party or of the Interested Party and (b) has a clear need to know for purposes directly related to the Matter. 2. Exclusions. The obligations of a Interested Party under Section 1.1 hereof shall not apply to the following: 2.1. Any Information which, at the time of disclosure, is already rightfully possessed by Interested Party or is already in the public domain; 2.2. Any Information which, after the disclosure, enters the public domain without any action or fault of Interested Party. 2.3. Any Information which is obtained, other than in connection with the Matter, from any individual, firm or entity which had the unrestricted right to disclose it; 2.4. Any Information which is developed independently by Interested Party without reference to any Information or Item;
perturbation.justification: The changed text redefines "Disclose" as "Disseminate" in the definition section. However, the contradicted text uses both "Disseminate" and "Disclose" but relies on the definition of "Disclose" when stating obligations in Section 1.1 ('not to disclosure any Information or Item'). Since "Disclose" is no longer defined (instead, disseminate is defined twice), this creates a contradiction because Section 1.1 refers to the undefined term "Disclose", while the changed text provides a definition for a term not used further in the document (disseminate).

perturbation.original_text: It includes but is not limited to ideas, inventions, discoveries, formulas, methods, designs, drawings, specifications, engineering and manufacturing data. This information is limited to trade secrets and other proprietary or private information of Disclosing Party or of any third party if disclosed by or through Disclosing Party.
perturbation.changed_text: It includes but is not limited to ideas, inventions, discoveries, formulas, methods, designs, drawings, specifications, engineering and manufacturing data. This information extends to all data possessed by the Disclosing Party or any third party if disclosed by or through Disclosing Party.
perturbation.explanation: By changing 'This information is limited to trade secrets and other proprietary or private information' to 'This information extends to all data possessed by the Disclosing Party', the scope of 'Information' becomes much broader. This contradicts the original intent of protecting only trade secrets. This intext contradiction is reflected throughout the text.
perturbation.contradicted_text: NON-DISCLOSURE AGREEMENT The parties to this Agreement are MPD Technologies, Inc. ("Disclosing Party") and the undersigned "Interested Party". The parties desire that Disclosing Party disclose certain Information or Items to Interested Party, but Disclosing Party desires to maintain the trade secret, proprietary or private nature of such Information or Items. As used herein, the following words have the indicated meanings: (i) "Disseminate" means to reveal, make known, make available, furnish, or permit access to, whether or not intentionally. (ii) "Information" means all oral, written, or other information whatsoever, including information in documents and other recording media and information embodied in any item, which in connection with the Matter, is (a) obtained by Interested Party from or through Disclosing Party, (b) obtained by or through Interested Party by an examination of any Item, or (c) created by or through Interested Party with the use of information in (a) or (b). It includes but is not limited to ideas, inventions, discoveries, formulas, methods, designs, drawings, specifications, engineering and manufacturing data. This information extends to all data possessed by the Disclosing Party or any third party if disclosed by or through Disclosing Party. (iii) "Item" means any system, subsystem, assembly, subassembly, device, components, product, or machine, work of authorship, or part thereof, or substance which is disclosed by or through Disclosing Party hereunder, which embodies trade secret or other proprietary or private information of Disclosing Party or of any third party if disclosed by or through Disclosing Party. (iv) "Matter" means the project or other matter in connection with which this Agreement is executed. This matter is or relates to the potential acquisition by the Interested Party of a controlling ownership interest in the share capital or business of the Disclosing Party. The parties to this Agreement have agreed as follows: 1. Non-Disclosure. Interested Party agrees as follows: 1.1. For a period of three (3) years from the date of entering in to this Agreement, not to disclosure any Information or Item, in any manner whatsoever, directly or indirectly, whether in whole or in part, without the prior written consent of Disclosing Party, to any individual, firm or entity, excepting solely an individual <PAGE> 2 who (a) is an officer, director or employee of Disclosing Party or of the Interested Party and (b) has a clear need to know for purposes directly related to the Matter. 2. Exclusions. The obligations of a Interested Party under Section 1.1 hereof shall not apply to the following: 2.1. Any Information which, at the time of disclosure, is already rightfully possessed by Interested Party or is already in the public domain; 2.2. Any Information which, after the disclosure, enters the public domain without any action or fault of Interested Party. 2.3. Any Information which is obtained, other than in connection with the Matter, from any individual, firm or entity which had the unrestricted right to disclose it; 2.4. Any Information which is developed independently by Interested Party without reference to any Information or Item;
perturbation.justification: The original text limits the 'Information' covered by the NDA to 'trade secrets and other proprietary or private information'. The changed text broadens the definition of 'Information' to include 'all data possessed by the Disclosing Party'. This creates a direct contradiction because the NDA now covers significantly more information than originally intended, conflicting with the desire to maintain only the trade secret, proprietary or private nature of such Information or Items throughout the rest of the document, particularly clause 1.1, where it states that the Interested Party must not disclose any 'Information', without prior consent.

perturbation.original_text: For a period of three (3) years from the date of entering in to this Agreement, not to disclosure any Information or Item, in any manner whatsoever, directly or indirectly, whether in whole or in part, without the prior written consent of Disclosing Party, to any individual, firm or entity
perturbation.changed_text: For a period of three (3) years from the date of entering in to this Agreement, not to disseminate any Information or Item, in any manner whatsoever, directly or indirectly, whether in whole or in part, without the prior written consent of Disclosing Party, to any individual, firm or entity
perturbation.explanation: Changing "disclose" to "disseminate" in Section 1.1 introduces a contradiction. Earlier, only 'Disseminate' was defined. Section 1.1 now uses 'disseminate', conflicting with the undefined 'disclose' used elsewhere. This creates uncertainty about the scope of obligations. This can lead to an intext contradiction, affecting the enforcement of the non-disclosure terms.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The parties to this Agreement have agreed as follows: 1. Non-Disclosure. Interested Party agrees as follows: 1.1. For a period of three (3) years from the date of entering in to this Agreement, not to disseminate any Information or Item, in any manner whatsoever, directly or indirectly, whether in whole or in part, without the prior written consent of Disclosing Party, to any individual, firm or entity, excepting solely an individual <PAGE> 2 who (a) is an officer, director or employee of Disclosing Party or of the Interested Party and (b) has a clear need to know for purposes directly related to the Matter. 2. Exclusions. The obligations of a Interested Party under Section 1.1 hereof shall not apply to the following: 2.1. Any Information which, at the time of disclosure, is already rightfully possessed by Interested Party or is already in the public domain; 2.2. Any Information which, after the disclosure, enters the public domain without any action or fault of Interested Party. 2.3. Any Information which is obtained, other than in connection with the Matter, from any individual, firm or entity which had the unrestricted right to disclose it; 2.4. Any Information which is developed independently by Interested Party without reference to any Information or Item;
perturbation.justification: The original text uses 'disclose,' which is undefined in the contradicted text, while the changed text uses 'disseminate,' which is defined in the contradicted text. The contradicted text in Section 1.1 uses 'disseminate.' The change to 'disseminate' in Section 1.1, where originally 'disclose' was used, introduces a contradiction because the document now inconsistently uses 'disclose' elsewhere, which is undefined. This is problematic because 'disseminate' is defined, but the new term 'disclose' is not, leading to ambiguity about what constitutes a breach of the agreement, according to the Misaligned Terminology definition provided.

perturbation.original_text: Notification of Mandatory Disclosure by Recipient. In the event you are required by any legal or civil proceeding to disclose the Information or Items to a third party, you agree to provide prompt notice to the Disclosing Party, so that we might attempt to secure an appropriate protective order, or waive your compliance with this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: Notification of Mandatory Divulgence by Interested Party. In the event you are required by any legal or civil proceeding to divulge the Information or Items to a third party, you agree to provide prompt notice to the Disclosing Party, so that we might attempt to secure an appropriate protective order, or waive your compliance with this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'Disclosure' to 'Divulgence' and 'disclose' to 'divulge' creates inconsistency since 'divulge' and 'Divulgence' are not defined terms within the agreement, leading to confusion about the nature of mandatory actions required from the recipient. 'Interested Party' replaced 'Recipient' from an earlier change. This creates an in-text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 4. Notification of Mandatory Divulgence by Interested Party. In the event you are required by any legal or civil proceeding to divulge the Information or Items to a third party, you agree to provide prompt notice to the Disclosing Party, so that we might attempt to secure an appropriate protective order, or waive your compliance with this Agreement. You agree to cooperate with the Disclosing Party in its attempts to secure such a protective order. 5. Only One Source of Discussion. You agree that you will only discuss the Matter with Mr. Al Weber, Chief Executive Officer, or Mr. Thomas V. Gilboy, Chief Financial Officer of the Disclosing Party.
perturbation.justification: The perturbation explanation states that changing 'Disclosure' to 'Divulgence' and 'disclose' to 'divulge' creates inconsistency because 'divulge' and 'Divulgence' are not defined terms. Replacing 'Recipient' with 'Interested Party', also creates an in-text contradiction, as stated in the Perturbation explanation. The Contradicted Text uses 'Divulgence' and 'Interested Party' which contradicts the original agreement, making it a Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction and thereby introducing a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Recipient shall not alter or delete any markings on any Information or Item restricting its disclosure, use or reproduction or indicating its proprietary or private status.
perturbation.changed_text: Interested Party shall not alter or delete any markings on any Information or Item restricting its dissemination, use or reproduction or indicating its proprietary or private status.
perturbation.explanation: By replacing 'disclosure' with 'dissemination,' while only 'disseminate' is defined, we create a contradiction. The meaning of markings restricting 'disclosure' is now unclear, as that action is not specifically defined. It can create an intext contradiction and make the agreement ambigious.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 8. Preservation of Markings. Interested Party shall not alter or delete any markings on any Information or Item restricting its dissemination, use or reproduction or indicating its proprietary or private status. Interested Party shall include all such markings in all copies of any Information which it makes. 9. No Proprietary Right; Return of Information or Items. Nothing herein shall confer upon Interested Party any right, title, interest or license whatsoever in or to any Information or Items. At the end of our discussions regarding the Matter, the Disclosing Party has the right to request that the Interested Party return all Information or Items to the Disclosing Party.
perturbation.justification: The original text refers to 'disclosure' while the changed text uses 'dissemination'. According to the perturbation explanation, 'disseminate' is the only defined term. The contradicted text uses 'dissemination'. The change from 'disclosure' to 'dissemination' creates ambiguity, as 'disclosure' is no longer present or defined anywhere which makes the term ambigious within the agreement. Because 'disclosure' is no longer a defined term, any markings restricting 'disclosure' are ambigious. Because of this, a strong in-text contradiction exists.
